Mr. Slutsky has been working on a probate case for 18 years, on 10/16/2003 Mr Slutsky filed the probate, in 18 years he has never provided any details of the inventory of the estate or gave real reasons why it should take 18 years to settle a estate other then nonsense reasons. 2003P008019 10/16/2003 Decedent Geraldine Newsome, filed Cook County Courthouse. He has been pleasant and nice, however it should not take 18 years to settle any estate without real answers.

My family and I have been clients of Jay Slutzky for 40 years. We have trusted Jay's expertise with everything from business and personal legal issues, to planning family trusts. We enjoy the confidence n knowing Jay is diligent in considering all options, explaining facts in understandable terms, and resolving the matter in a quick and appropriate time frame. I find Jay to be a sharp and professional attorney, yet has always returned my calls and treats me in a respectful and humane manor. I never hesitate to recommend Jay to family members and friends.

Very professional and thorough. Listens really good, checks in on you and offers you good advice along with giving you options. Would highly recommend Jay Slutzky’s Law Firm. Been a customer for over 25 years. Would not trust anyone else with my financial affairs.

Jay A. Slutzky he is an excellent attorney who is very hard-working, extremely knowledgeable, and professional. I would absolutely recommend this firm to anyone considering filing for bankruptcy.

I don’t know where to start. Robert and his staff are phenomenal. During the worst time of our lives Robert and his staff were so kind, compassionate and supportive. My mom was killed by an intoxicated driver and my dad was incapacitated. My sister and I had to deal with grieving our mom, grieving our dad as we knew him, court proceedings, manage finances, etc. Robert never added any stress to our already full plates. He always promptly responded to our questions and contacted us to discuss every decision we needed to make. He was always completely honest with us. He never strung us along and only had our best interest in mind. Robert truly cares for his clients and I will never forget some of the kind words he said when we’d be on conference calls crying and telling him how we felt. We were in a very vulnerable time in our lives and we could not have asked for a better lawyer. I feel like Robert is a friend and not just our lawyer. Robert was an angel on Earth during an impossible time. I know my mom would’ve loved him. Choose the Rooth Law Firm-you will never find better.
An excellent, professional, and effective lawyer. Mr. Robert resolved my case in a timely manner and I was very satisfied with the outcome. I appreciate his exceptional work and professionalism."

My personal injury case was handled with unwavering compassion and support from start to finish.  There was particular care given to understanding my residual physical limitations and pain.  I always felt my interests were protected with Robert as my advocate in securing the very best outcome for me.  Robert had also successfully fought a nursing home neglect case of a beloved family member.  I highly recommend The Rooth Law Firm!

After a frustrating experience with another law firm, I contacted Attorney Robert Rooth’s office for help with my father’s nursing home case—on a tight timeline. From the start, Mr. Rooth and Jessica were absolutely phenomenal. He listened to my concerns, jumped into action immediately, and kept me informed every step of the way.

Unlike my previous experience, I never felt like just another case. Mr. Rooth made me feel like my case truly mattered. I always felt supported and prepared, and it was clear he genuinely cared about getting the best outcome.

I can’t recommend him enough for nursing home or personal injury cases. I hope I never need an attorney again, but if I do, I’ll be calling him without hesitation!

I highly highly highly recommend Jack Rubin of the Rooth Law Firm. He represented my mom in a personal injury case after a car accident where the other driver was at fault (under the influence). He took care of everything in a very thorough, professional, supportive, and timely way with quick, amazing results. It was such a comfort to us as she was going through the recovery process. Thanks again, Jack!

If I could give 10 stars I would! Robert and his associate Jack Rubin were so helpful with my mother's nursing home fall/negligence case. I was referred to Robert by a friend of mine and I'm so grateful for the referral.  The day of my mom's fall I called Robert unbeknownst to me that he was on vacation with his family. I left a message for him and within minutes Robert had called me back and the first thing he asked was is my mom ok and how she was doing. Robert and his associates bring back that human touch that is missing nowadays. He explained everything step by step as to how things would progress and kept me informed the entire time. I was extremely happy with the outcome of the case and extremely thankful for Robert's expertise. I hope that I will never have to use their services again, but if I do ...The Rooth Law Firm is at the top of my list.

I am so happy that we selected Rooth Law Firm and the team to represent us. From the beginning, they were supportive, kind, great listeners, and worked diligently on our case. We felt like they really cared about our case and our needs. Rob and his very talented team kept us informed on a consistent basis and ensured there was complete understanding in all situations before moving on to something else. We highly recommend this firm to handle your needs. You will not be disappointed.
The Rooth Law Firm.

Throughout this journey, our relationship went far beyond that of an attorney and client — we became like family, like brothers. He supported me not only legally but also morally, and I always felt that he genuinely cared about my case and my future.
From start to finish, he handled everything with great professionalism, kept me informed at every step, and made sure I was never alone during this stressful process. Thanks to his experience, dedication, and commitment, my green card was finally approved.
I am truly grateful to him and the entire HELP Law Offices team.
I highly recommend them to anyone going through an immigration process.

reviews.text:
First of all, I have never had any personal contact with Mr. Seyit Ali Sahin, so I cannot say whether he is professional in his work or not. I chose Help Law Office upon a friend’s recommendation. We agreed on the contract price, and I made all my payments on time as we had discussed.

One day, I needed assistance regarding an immigration matter. They had created a WhatsApp group for me. Despite messaging them multiple times, no one ever replied or called me back. When I later told one of the office employees that I would not make any more payments because I was dissatisfied with their service, he called me and spoke in a rude and unprofessional manner. I responded accordingly, but there should never be such behavior in a professional service environment.

After that incident, I stopped making payments. Some time later, I received a call from someone I believe to be the office manager. I explained my dissatisfaction, and he told me that the employee in question had been fired and that “everything will be fine, blah blah blah.” He convinced me to resume payments.

The very next day, I called the office again with another immigration question. A new female employee answered, told me she would call back shortly to address my question—and, of course, she never called back. After several attempts, I once again got no response. Seeing such unprofessional behavior, I decided to completely end my cooperation with that office.

Nine months later, I suddenly received another call from them saying, “You haven’t paid us.” Can you imagine? After nine months! During that time, my EAD card had expired, and I had already paid another paralegal to renew it. Yet they still claimed that “our office has been monitoring your case and everything is in order.” A complete lie.

I informed the manager that I was no longer working with them and had chosen a new attorney. Finally, they tried one last time to squeeze money out of me, saying, “You still owe us $700.” In reality, I received no real service for all the payments I had already made.

In conclusion:
If you are an immigrant looking for truly professional service, find yourself a proper law office—otherwise, you’ll only end up dealing with frustration and wasted money like I did.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are sorry to hear that you are dissatisfied with your experience. We take all client concerns seriously and strive to provide professional and timely service to every individual we represent. Due to our attorney-client confidentiality obligations, we are unable to address or respond to specific allegations or timelines in a public forum.
We respectfully note that our office handles a high volume of immigration matters and follows internal procedures regarding communication, case monitoring, and billing. When concerns are brought to our attention, we make every effort to review them in good faith and address them appropriately.
We encourage you to contact our office directly so that any outstanding issues can be reviewed through proper channels. We remain committed to maintaining professional standards and treating all clients with respect.

Should have done this a while ago. A huge thank you to Help us Here, Back in December 2021, I ended up in a mess made by my uncle. He was never married, no children and could not care for himself. He had neglected to pay billls and had run up a $13,000 credit card bill due to late fees and interest, among other things. He had a $1000 a month SSA payment and some retirement accounts. With the cost of his care in assisted living nearing $8000 a month, I had no idea how I would be able to keep him there and pay his debts. I stumbled across HELPS  online and decided to contact them. The went over his situation with me and determined that they could help without him having to declare bankruptcy. Essentially, they contacted his creditors and kept them at bay. In May of 2024, my uncle passed away. Two months prior, I received a notice from Discover that his debt was forgiven.  Helps asked for a copy of the death certificate so they could inform his creditors.The other creditors have vanished.
.
If you need professional support, you are definitely in the wrong place. I do not recommend it to anyone.

reviews.text:
I am the kind of person that never buys anything without reading the reviews first (I’m in the process of purchasing a lot of stuff now, details to follow). So, if you’re reading this, we have something in common. I will try and be as succinct as possible (almost impossible for me) The date was September 26, 2024. I live in Saint Petersburg Florida and the reports were coming in that hurricane Helene would hit my area. I have been living here for 8 years, so after living through 8 years of hurricane seasons and hearing about the big one that would hit Tampa Bay this year, I shrugged my shoulders and decided to stay in my house. That evening the winds were blowing the waves in the bay crashing against the sea walls. No big deal, I’ve seen it before. My wife, my dog and I were all tucked in bed safely knowing that my full house generator would kick in if the winds got too bad. All of a sudden, I started getting cold and wet and realized that there was water up to the top of my bed. UH OH.
At day break after the hurricane subsided and I was able to get to my front door. I realized that we had over 3 feet of water in the house. This was the most catastrophic event in my life and in Tampa Bays life. I quickly called my friends to come and rescue us. My cars were flooded and we were trapped.
My brother called me that morning to see how we were doing. One of his best friends is a 20+ year retired public adjuster (I had never even heard of one). His exact words to me were… “Don’t even think about dealing with your insurance company without hiring a public adjuster”. He gave me Ron’s number at A. Schoeneman & Co.
This had to be the best advice I had ever gotten from my big brother (I am in my 60’s); I could not imagine dealing with all of this without the help of Ron and his team. I watched all of my friends going crazy dealing with their insurance companies (writing lists of their stuff etc.…) I also listened to some of the settlements they were getting. How would I rebuild? Ron’s team came to my house the day my insurance companies’ adjuster was there to evaluate my losses. The details of my belongings being logged for hours upon hours.
Once I saw the report that Ron and his team submitted to my insurance company, I finally was able to breath. The details were astounding! Finally, the settlement came in. It was pretty much exactly what I “valued” my belongings at.
So, if you jumped to the end of this review. Here is my recommendation to you. If you made it through your catastrophic event and are ready to call your insurance company.  Stop reading this and pick up the phone and Call A. Schoeneman & Co. you will not be sorry. This is what they do. They speak insurance (trust me you don’t)! I have given Ron permission to give out my phone number if you want to speak to me about my experience.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the kind words. Good luck to you and your family as you start to rebuild.   My team will be there every step of the way for you

Justin Schwartz was hired by my family to represent my sister in a murder case, and he was paid half of his cost which was $15,000.  He was paid $7,500 from my family and at first he seemed to be on top of the business when he represented her at her sentencing.  He even won her appeal, but when it was time to put in work he vanished, stating that he needed the remaining of his pay.  However, when we talked he knew that our family was struggling and that he accepted the 7500.  But he did not go to court to represent her in appeals.  He stop answering my calls, text, and emails.  Each time I tried to contact him he has excuses and all he would say when I did catch a chance to talk to him is that he can't do anything else for her until he get all his money.  But remind you, He got $7500 from us in the beginning.  Now my sister case has been taken out of appeals and she had to file a post conviction due to misrepresentation and inactive support from Justin Schwartz.  He was brought up on charges and appeared in court himself for ineffective counsel.  He is not a good lawyer, all he want is the money but don't want to do the work.  Now my sister is suffering locked up and waiting for effective representation.

Attorney Dave and Paralegal Sam are both AMAZING!!! I had a simple case (so my spouse and I agreed on all terms ahead of time), but I knew I’d need help to deal with the VERY TRICKY court paperwork and court specifics. Before finding this firm, I already met with 2 attorneys from different firms, discussed my situation & what I needed (basically told them “my spouse & I agree, can you file things properly so this gets done as quick as possible??”)

BOTH of the other firms wanted a $5000 RESERVING fee despite KNOWING my case was “simple”….and of course if they worked longer, they’d charge more hourly if they ran through that. When they ask for that much for a simple case TO START, I felt they’d try to milk me for as much as possible. So I even considered buying one of those online court paperwork fillers & filing MYSELF…but knew that ONE TINY error would delay my case or have it thrown out even! I felt so scared and hopeless!

That’s when I found THIS firm, and I’m SO BEYOND GRATEFUL I did!!! Dave and Sam take care of EVERYTHING, with only one SMALL and REASONABLE fee!!!

They made the entire process smooth as silk!!! They also both answered all my questions right away whenever I texted or emailed! Sam especially has been SO SUPPORTIVE and kind, she knew I was anxious about this process so she always texted and called me within 5 mns of me reaching out to her! She’s just a wonderful person!

I KNOW this is a scary and heartbreaking process for anyone, and I felt so alone and anxious until I found this firm. So I’m here to tell you that YOU don’t have to feel that way. Hire THIS firm to take care of you, so you don’t have to worry about ANYTHING. Their flat fee is so affordable and they’ve done this to try to help those of us who know our case is simple but don’t want to deal with the anxiety of filing and perfecting tricky court paperwork. I’m so grateful for Dave and Sam!

reviews.text: After a long nightmarish process of trying to sift through all the options to find an affordable honest divorce lawyer….in the midst of the usual exhaustion, confusion, and burnout that comes with being a single parent going through such a challenging time, I had the good fortune of stumbling across Dave Wolkowitz’s law office in a google search.  I called him, he returned my call later that day and was very straightforward, not at all slick like so many other attorneys, open about discussing the outrageous prices other attorneys were quoting me and why that was, and gave me a realistic timeline and all the information I needed to move forward with my uncontested divorce case.   I paid a flat reasonable fee up front, and after a few months of being sent the necessary documents electronically, signing them along with my ex, and communicating with his paralegal Sam, we had our prove up hearing online, and I was awarded my divorce.  I am extremely grateful that Dave presented himself as an option because I don’t know what I would have done otherwise.  His competition who  offer the exact same services were literally quoting me five times what I paid.  It makes me sad that so many in the industry  would take advantage of struggling mothers during such a vulnerable time, but such is life and our rapacious system.  I would highly recommend Dave to anyone I know who needs a fair and honest lawyer, and to all of this reading this, best of luck with your divorce.

I have been a client of Lee's for the past 7 years.  I had a very contentious divorce that led to me having to readdress a number of post decree issues for the health and safety of my minor child.  After working with no less than 3 previous firms, with little positive outcome, I found Lee Zeidman.  Lee is a no nonsense straight talking attorney who refuses to get spun up with all of the drama that is so typical of Family Courts.  Lee's knowledge of IL family law is unmatched and his desire to seek solutions instead of delay them is a very bright spot in an otherwise dark place. End of day, I was awarded custody of my minor child due to Lee's approach and determination to see things right.  More than one attorney told me that it simply wasn't possible ( especially in DuPage County) for a father to gain custody of a minor,,, well, they were wrong.
Fast forward 3 years and once again : Lee Zeidman to the rescue!
I was involved in another very complex business dispute with my partners and  Lee stepped in to help guide me through, and lead the process toward a very favorable outcome for myself and family.  Who knew?? The best Family Law attorney that I have dealt with also has major chops in business and contract litigation.  Lee is also extremely knowledgeable in trust and estate planning,,, so for the trifecta,,, Lee and myself sat down and re-did my entire estate plan and updated my needs to reflect updated IL estate planning law.  All told, Lee is my one and only attorney and he earned that right through his knowledge, his shrewdness and his tenacity.
If your looking for one of Chicago's best attorneys, congratulations, your search has ended!!!  Lee is a cut above all of the rest.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts, Emerald. While we appreciate feedback, we also believe it's important to clarify that a retainer is not a flat fee. It is an upfront deposit held in trust and applied toward actual work performed on a case. In child support matters, especially those involving disputes, financial disclosures, and potential litigation, the legal complexity often justifies such a retainer.

We understand legal fees can feel high, but they reflect the seriousness of the work, the stakes involved, and the depth of experience we bring to each case. We are always happy to explain our billing structure to anyone genuinely interested in legal representation.

— Zeidman & Carpenter PLLC

We worked with Lee on estate planning to help us set up a trust. He is extremely knowledgeable and walked us through all the steps so we felt comfortable during the entire process. He was accommodating to our tight turnaround and we were able to get everything competed on time.

This is coming from an actual client of Mr. Lee Zeidman, so please read carefully. Mr. Zeidman charged me over $18,000 over a course of 9 months handling (or dare I say mishandling) my divorce case.

I hired Mr. Lee Zeidman in early Feb 2023 as my divorce attorney. He started with $4500 retainer. As my case progressed it appeared to me he did not know what he was doing. For example, my spouse had been a working individual all 16 years of marriage and decided not to work after filing to give me a hard time. I asked Mr. Zeidman at several occasions to find a legal way to make her look for a job. He said there was no way and gave me many excuses how it was not possible. (I later learned that all an attorney needs to do is to file a motion to compel to work). Due to Lee Zeidman’s inability, I had to pay my spouse exorbitant sums for support which would otherwise be unnecessary. So much so, her attorney argued and compelled me to pay her attorney’s fees also since she didn’t have a job and Mr. Zeidman couldn’t do anything about it. Don’t forget, I was also paying for mortgage on the house where I no longer lived on top of support. To add insult to my injury, when my financial records were subpoenaed by my Ex’s attorney, both of these attorneys (mine and hers) were charging me at attorney’s rate to review those financial records. When I objected and asked Lee Zeidman to ask for court appointed independent financial expert, he had no idea what to do. Mr. Zeidman’s response was that each party had a right to have these records reviewed “whichever way they wanted”. Last but not least, I kept asking Lee Zeidman to have appraisals done on all property while he kept dragging it for reasons unbeknownst to me.

Make long story short after working with Lee Zeidman for 9 months and spending more than $18,000, I was getting nowhere. My spouse was still not working, no financial expert had been hired and no appraisals were done while Mr. Zeidman kept charging me legal fees for useless work that was producing no results. At last, I came to my senses and let go Mr. Zeidman in late Oct 2023 and finally hired an attorney who knew what he was doing. He started to produce results immediately using the law to my advantage. My spouse was working in no time, financial expert, called 503(I) expert was hired at reasonable rates, my soon to be EX started paying her own attorney’s fees and appraisals got ordered. Most distressing part in dealing with Lee Zeidman was that I kept asking him what to do, while he kept ignoring me either due to gross negligence or just sheer incompetence.

As painful as divorce is, Mr. Zeidman just added to my misery many folds. He charged me hefty legal sums for work that produced NO RESULTS. I don’t know how much more he would have dragged this on, had I not had the foresight to cut ties with him and hire someone else.

STAY AWAY FROM LEE ZEIDMAN AND ZEIDMAN & CARPENTER!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dissolution of marriage, especially with minor children, is a very emotional, personal, and difficult time for a client. Thankfully, in most cases, we are able to meet our clients' expectations, achieve their goals, and reach a conclusion that allows them to move forward in life, focusing their time, hard-earned money, and energy on their families and future.

While many of the statements made in this review are factually inaccurate and not corroborated by the actual circumstances, case facts, file records, and communications, they reflect the client's feelings today, 9 months after last being represented by our firm.

As a business, it is never ideal to have someone so upset that they return 9 months later to leave a scathing review of their experiences. Syed, as we have communicated via email in the last couple of weeks, we are sorry that the relationship deteriorated and ultimately ended the way it did. However, your statements are factually incorrect, and no amount of ‘evidence’ we present will likely satisfy your emotions.

We wish you the best of luck going forward.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear David G,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with us. We greatly appreciate all feedback, as it helps us improve our services and enhance the overall client experience. However, it appears there may have been a slight misunderstanding in your interaction with our firm.

We sincerely apologize if you felt that our receptionist hung up on you without uttering a customary salutation. Rest assured, this is not our standard practice. We pride ourselves on being professional and courteous to all callers, whether they are prospective clients or not. While we acknowledge that we may not have met your expectations on this occasion, we would like to assure you that this incident is an anomaly, and we have taken immediate steps to rectify the situation.

In this digital age, we rely heavily on phone calls to communicate with our clients and potential clients. We value each and every call that comes through our lines and understand the importance of providing an excellent first impression. It is unfortunate that our receptionist did not meet our usual high standards during your interaction.

We have conducted a thorough investigation into the matter and have retrained our staff to ensure that such a lapse in professionalism does not occur again. We take our commitment to exceptional client service seriously, and we are dedicated to continually improving our processes and communication.

We would like to invite you to reconsider your decision and provide us with another opportunity to assist you. Should you wish to discuss your legal matter further, please feel free to contact us at: Info@ZeidmanCarpenter.com We would be more than happy to offer our expertise and provide you with the assistance you require.

Once again, we apologize for any inconvenience or frustration caused, and we appreciate your understanding. We genuinely value your feedback, as it allows us to grow and better serve our clients in the future.

Kind regards,

Zeidman & Carpenter PLLC

I cannot recommend Azita highly enough. She helped me successfully obtain my U.S. citizenship, and the entire process was smooth and stress-free because of her expertise and dedication.

From start to finish, Azita was professional, organized, and extremely knowledgeable about immigration law. She made sure every document was prepared correctly and took the time to explain each step clearly. Anytime I had questions, she responded promptly and made me feel confident that everything was under control.

I also want to thank her incredible team, Roxy and Sasha. They were always helpful, responsive, and supportive throughout the entire process. Their communication and attention to detail made everything move efficiently and gave me peace of mind.

If you are looking for the best immigration lawyer, Azita and  her team are the people you want representing you. They truly care about their clients and go above and beyond to ensure success. I’m incredibly grateful and would recommend them to anyone going through the immigration process. Becoming a U.S. citizen is one of the most meaningful milestones of my life, and I will always appreciate the role they played in helping me achieve this dream.

I’ve been working with Azita’s team for a long time and it’s been great to have such an awesome team to working with. Responsive, knowledgeable and supportive are just few of the qualities that her office provides. Whatever your situation is don’t hesitate to reach out her office for a consultation.

They are great at what they do!!!

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough about the incredible experience I had working with Azita Mojarad.
She helped me bring my parents from Persia to the United States and guided us all the way through to their citizenship during one of the hardest times of our lives.
What makes her truly unique is not only her deep intelligence and expertise in immigration law, but also the way she genuinely cares about her clients. She took the time to listen to our story, understand our challenges, and craft solutions that worked for us. Every step of the way, she explained the process clearly and made us feel supported and reassured.
Her professionalism and dedication are unmatched. She combines sharp legal skills with compassion and patience—qualities that are rare to find together. Thanks to her, my parents are U.S. citizens.
I would recommend Azita Mojarad without hesitation to anyone in need of an immigration attorney. She is a remarkable lawyer and an even more remarkable person.

The immigration process is very difficult. This is where the expertise of Azita and her team comes in. You need a lawyer who responds to emails in a timely manner, works hard for her clients, and is committed to getting results for their cases — Azita does exactly that. I had a bad experience with another lawyer who wouldn’t respond to me for days; Azita and her team are the complete opposite.
Azita is my lawyer handling my immigration case, and it’s a challenging one. However, I truly believe Azita will resolve it.

What’s so great about this law firm is that you don’t need to follow up with them — they immediately follow up with you once actions are taken on your case. Not many lawyers do that. Most make you wait for their response, but this law office is the opposite: you get updates as soon as they are available.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your generous 5-star review! We will continue to do our best to exceed your expectations and achieve a favorable resolution. We’re always here to help.

Like many people, I’ve had my fair share of experiences with firms that didn’t seem to truly care—firms that felt like they were just there to collect a check. That completely changed when I started working with Marvin & Associates.

Jim Schiff was assigned to my Social Security case, and I honestly don’t even know where to begin. Jim wasn’t just excellent with communication—his patience, knowledge, and professionalism were truly above and beyond anything I’ve ever experienced. From day one, he genuinely made me feel like he had my best interests at heart. Every time I had a question, he didn’t just respond quickly—he took the time to explain things thoroughly and educate me, never once making me feel like my questions were silly or unimportant. His patience and expertise clearly showed that he truly knows what he’s doing.

While the outcome of my case was very positive, I can honestly say the journey getting there was even better because of Jim. On our final call, I was so pleased with my experience that I asked if Marvin & Associates could also represent me in a workers’ compensation case. Unfortunately, they only handle Social Security matters—but that alone says a lot about how much trust I had in them.

Fast forward a couple of years later, I received a letter from Social Security that I didn’t fully understand. I reached out to Jim, hoping he might be able to clarify it or at least point me in the right direction. That same day, Jim called me personally. Not only did he take the time to explain the letter in detail, but he also clearly remembered specifics of my case from years earlier and explained exactly what the letter meant for my situation.

How many people do you know who take so much pride in their work that they remember small details about a client years later? That truly impressed me and completely reinforced my belief that Jim genuinely cares about people and isn’t just doing this for a paycheck.

If you need help with Social Security, I cannot recommend Marvin & Associates enough. And if you’re able to, ask for Jim Schiff—he is truly an asset to the company.

Jim’s expertise, persistence, and experience working with such agencies, ultimately rendered a decision from SSDI that not only absolved me of any financial burden, but resulted in a reinstatement of benefits, as well as back pay for months payment was lost. I would recommend his counsel to anybody looking to navigate SSDI issues.  Fees were more than fair.  Thank you, Jim!

reviews.text: There are many honest real estate attorneys in the Chicago land area and Shapiro is not one of them.  My entire family used Shapiro and we will avoid going forward.  In some cases, the assessments can naturally reduce (e.g. COVID time frame).  However, his office will take credit in an effort to maximize his profitability.  Extremely unethical.  If you protest or ask for explanation / clarity, they will threaten legal action.  I believe another review alluded to similar concerns.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Irman, our office has never charged for an appeal reduction that we didn't achieve. During covid, when a lot of assessments were reduced by the Assessor's Office, we only billed if we were able to achieve a further reduction. When we bill, we always send an official result letter from either the Cook County Assessor or Board of Review, depending on which agency we appealed with. The result letter will detail the reduction the attorney achieved, only. And we bill based on our efforts.

You can verify if a reduction was achieved by an attorney by contacting the Assessor or Board of Review or you can go on their websites and enter your PIN. When you search under "appeal history" you can see if the attorney is listed as the appellant. So, you don't have to take our word for it if you are curious if the attorney achieved a reduction or if it was a covid reduction.

As far as another reviewer stating that we threaten legal action for asking for clarity, I am not seeing what you are seeing.

We have been in business over 32 years and have never threatened anyone with legal action when a client asks for clarity. In the 43 years since Richard has been admitted to the Illinois Bar to practice law, there have been no record of any discipline by the Attorney Registration & Disciplinary Commission of the Supreme Court of Illinois. We are, also, rated A+ by the Better Business Bureau.

If we were a law office that operated unethically, I don't believe we would have the volume of positive reviews that we do; that reputation would have caught up to us many years ago.

Best of luck to you in your future appeals.

reviews.text:
I and my uncle have used Richard shapiro for years. However, it come to my attention that in the last 2 years their has  not been  service that equal fees he charges. My recommendation is not to use him. Furthermore, do your research and find out if he's actually really giving you a reduction in your taxes. Attorneys like him are falsifying documentation  that is not legitimate to the city of Chicago publication of tax ID OF YOUR PROPERTY.

warning:. Do your homework !
reviews.ownerResponse: Dick, I don't know where to begin with your allegations. We charge based on the fees in the signed contract. When we invoice our clients, an official result letter from either the Assessor or the Board of Review is sent in the results packet. This information is easily verifiable by confirming the appeal win on either the Cook County Board of Review website or the Assessor's website. So, yes, you are correct. You can do your homework to verify if we actually won your appeal and if you are being invoiced based on the fees in the contract. It would just take a few minutes of research.

reviews.text:
I would recommend avoiding his services. I experienced very poor client communication, little effort, poor results and questionable practices.  Shapiro’s results were very poor and I wish to share information in this regard. I was very dissatisfied with Mr. Shapiro’s obtaining a relatively small reduction ($58,000) on my behalf in the appeal of the 2018 triennial reassessment and with his handling of the appeal.   I appealed the very same reassessment amount by myself to the Cook County Board of Appeals for the 2019 tax year and (with no change in the property or other facts) on 4/1/20, obtained an additional $99,000 reduction, equaling almost half of the original 2018 $214,000 (50%!) increased reassessment—an additional reduction that Mr. Shapiro has repeatedly claimed was impossible.  While it’s true that Shapiro got 99% of what he sought on his initial appeal of the 2018 triennial reassessment, the problem was that he:
1) asked for ONLY a one quarter reduction of the $214,000 increase in my assessment (without my knowledge or consent), and
2) put forth narrowly-focused, minimal efforts to show that I had been greatly over-assessed.
The way Shapiro handled my case made it extremely difficult to appeal the initial decision to a higher level and ended my ability to further challenge the full three year triennial reassessment.  I was left with the ability to appeal the reassessment for the individual last two years of the triennial (2019 & 2020). The Assessor’s Office confirmed that I need to file a 2020 appeal only as a formality and will retain the reduction I obtained for 2019.  :-)

In Mr. Shapiro’s response to my complaint with the Better Business Bureau, he argues that further appeals of my case would never have succeeded:

“He [me, the client & reviewer] refused to accept that all that could have been done was done and that we did as well as could be expected.  It was just not what he wanted to hear.
***The reduction given to the subject property was consistent with, if not considerably better than, other assessments in his neighborhood.*** As a practical matter this eliminated the possibility let alone likelihood of any further reduction.***In my opinion, all those comparables on the block being higher fatally doomed a further appeal, and it doesn’t matter if my client would have had a great case to be made with properties in the section or quarter-section.  No colorable rationale rose close to the level of justifying an appeal.”

And yet, my appeal, on the same facts, in the next year got a $99,000 assessment additional reduction--nearly half of the entire increase and nearly double what Shapiro obtained in 2018.  I obtained the very large reduction by submitting a thorough analysis of comparable properties, which reveals the kind of cookie-cutter, minimalist representation Shapiro provided me.  Very few people know enough about how the property tax system has worked and are grateful for any reduction at all, but don’t realize they may be foregoing thousands in reductions that could be obtained by vigorous representation.  I would be wary of lawyers, such as Shapiro, who work on a contingency basis, and recommend those who charge a flat fee.   I also would be wary of past positive reviews--the new Cook County Assessor's rules require all appeals to be filed anonymously without attorney names. A property tax attorney who I considered hiring indicated that the new rules have made life quite difficult for certain property tax lawyers, perhaps including Mr Shapiro, who (to obtain favorable treatment) relied on political contributions to the Cook County Board of Review and to the prior Assessor.

I'm so sorry I used him on a neighbor's advice.  Except as a client, I have no relationship with a property tax attorney and no financial interest in this matter other than my own personal property taxes.
reviews.ownerResponse: Robert, thanks for your feedback. While you are entitled to your own opinion, I do not agree with your claims. I disagree with your position that your results were poor, since we won your 2018 appeal and got 99% of what we asked for as far as a reduction in your assessed value. As far as our communication with you, we did email you four status updates for your 2018 appeal. Also, you have spoken to us on numerous occasions with respect to your questions about the appeal, so I am not sure what other communication you are looking for. And, finally, your claim that we put forth minimal effort is just not factual. Selecting the evidence to submit for an appeal is something that we have over 26 years of experience with; it takes skill and effort, and we feel we do it well. I wish you the best of luck in your future appeals and hope you find what you are looking for.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Greetings Mr. Farooq. I apologize if you had to wait. My consultations unfortunately regularly go over the normal 30 minute consultation time, as I hate to cut people off, who are discussing very personal and sensitive matters. This unfortunately throws the rest of the consultations off. Most wait patiently, as it becomes apparent to them, that each client is being given ample time, and no one is being rushed.

You haven't hired us for legal work, and are basing your one star review and comments to the public based on your waiting time. You state: "I wouldn't recommend this office to anyone looking for reliable and respectful legal services."

That's a pretty strong statement when you are reviewing a professional. Its one thing to review McDonalds, if they took too long to give you your food.

For professional services, would be nice if there was some depth to the review aside from waiting time. Waiting time issues are caused by a variety of factors, some controllable, and some not. But the quality of our work, the efficiency of our staff, and the dedication of all of our team members, can only be gauged after the consultation and upon hiring us.

And particularly when you give a negative review to a solo practitioner, who's life's work has been to help people facing immigration related issues. I'll be honest, its a little hurtful.

Anyways, I hope you end up finding competent help at an office where they may get you in closer to your appointment time. Good Luck!

I've lived in the neighborhood for 55 years- all my life. I had  circumstances that caused me to live off of credit, and I got overwhelmed. I kept paying thousands in interest alone, for years. I made the payments on time though. But my balances, they weren't budging.
I just Googled "Bankruptcy Attorney Near Me" one day, out of desperate curiosity. I'm Polish, he's Polish, it's a match.

His office is easy to find, I take Milwaukee and park right on Menard, in front of the hair salons. See photo.

Robert is punctual, kind, extremely well mannered and professional. Almost too professional, I thought.. this guy is all business and a tough nut to crack! But he is efficient, he is writing the email as you speak so it is in your box before you leave his office. He does what he promises, right away. No secretary, and he ALWAYS answers and responds ASAP.
He is busy, and he is smart, So he is surgically precise in his actions. I understood everything he told me, every step of the way. He made me feel safe, relived, and informed. He did not make me feel awful for my situation, in fact I felt not so alone anymore and the embarrassment and guilt melted away, with each ensuing visit. I had to get my records in order, because Robert does not play with people's lives- he helps restore them with dignity. THAT is worth EVERY penny, which, is *Nothing* compared to the $60k I owed.
As we met 4 times over a 3 month period, Robert started to open up little by little. He is clever, skillful and genuinely kind hearted. At month 4 my records were ready. He told me 1 month prior. My meeting with the trustee was over in about 90 seconds, as he said it'd be. I was more than prepared by Robert the day before, to answer a range of questions, most of which he said would not be asked, and he was right again.
My Ch7 is discharged without contest, mid July 2025.
I have no further action to take than to follow his SIMPLE AND EASY, SHORT directions on how to regain excellent credit. I am already at 685, and my meeting was just 11 days ago.
Still, I feel comfortable picking up the phone to call him.
Do not put your drink on his handmade desk without a coaster.
Come prepared with every statement from every account, your Soc Sec card, your DL and paystubs. Tell him EVERYTHING.
You will instantly feel better.
If you're stuck, stop struggling. It doesn't matter why, just do it sooner than later. Had I done it 2-3 years prior, I would have saved THOUSANDS of dollars. I just wanted to make right with my debt, but the interest, was killing me. It's kiling you too if you're reading this.
Call Robert, he's got a proven plan! Feel better, get your life back on track without feeling worse than you do now- about all of it.

reviews.text:
We used Gambacorta Law for my mom's citizenship application. The reason why I'm giving them a 1 start is because of their assistant, Loan. From my 2 years interaction with her, I feel like she is dishonest. She is not upfront about charges. I was told this is the cost for the whole process. Then additional cost keep popping up. She should have been upfront about it. It ends up being an additional 2k for us. I let it slides since we're grateful for their help. However, my last straw was when Loan promised me that their office will apply for my mom's social security benefits for free. A month later after giving her all the information she needs, I called her to check the status. And she told me that she gave it to another person to do it. That person will apply for "free." However, he will need to take my mom to the interview and then he will charge money. He hasn't even contacted us. The reason why we agreed to let her apply is because she made it seemed like it will be done online through the lawyer group. If we had know, it was just going to be like us apply for it ourselves, we could have done it a month ago.

To sum it up, before doing business with them, please ask them about hidden charges. Even if the contract said they will not charge any additional fees, the "contractors they use" will definitely charge additional fees.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Casey,

I am deeply saddened to read your recent review, as it contains inaccuracies that compel me to address certain confidential matters in order to defend the integrity of our legal services. Your mother engaged our office to assist with her naturalization case.

Due to some life changes in Vietnam, she faced certain challenges and was unable to study for the naturalization exam and grasping an understanding of the written/spoken English language . After a referral to a medical professional, we submitted a report to USCIS with their findings. At both interviews, USCIS questioned the credibility of her past medical history affecting her test preparation. We diligently attended these interviews as per the representation agreement, taking detailed contemporaneous notes that highlight our advocacy on your mother's behalf. Our commitment extended beyond these obligations, as we advanced an appeal at no additional charge, aside from application fees, as outlined in our agreement. Ultimately, our efforts culminated in a successful appeal, and your mother is now a proud citizen.

Your allegation that our office "is not upfront about charges" is totally baseless and misleading. The signed representation agreement explicitly detailed the scope of our services, including attorney fees and any out-of-pocket expenses. Furthermore, your claim of hiring us for "social security benefits" is incorrect. The agreement solely covered naturalization proceedings, and we do not offer representation for social security matters. We referred you to an outside document preparer for assistance with social security benefits, without any financial gain on our part. Moreover, your suggestion that contractors will charge additional fees contradicts the clarity of our agreement, which specifies any government application fees or other out-of-pocket expenses. We do not engage outside contractors for our legal work. All legal work per our contract was accomplished within our law firm. We DO NOT prepare Social Security documentation as this is outside our scope of immigration services. The referral to a document preparer for social security services remains distinct from our contracted legal services. I urge you to reconsider your statements, as they may have serious legal implications due to their misleading nature and defamatory speak of our associate Ms. Loan.

Review for Jonathan Minkus and Carol

I had the privilege of working with Attorney Jonathan Minkus and his secretary, Carol, on a post-conviction relief case that had been haunting my life for over 30 years. From the moment I reached out, Jonathan's approach was nothing short of exceptional. He is direct, honest, and firm—qualities that are often hard to find in legal representation. His knowledge and professionalism gave me a sense of clarity and confidence during a time when I had nearly lost hope.

What stood out the most was Jonathan's unwavering honesty. He didn’t make promises he couldn’t keep, and he provided a realistic outlook on the process. Unlike others, who may have given false hope, Jonathan remained steadfast in his commitment to handling my case with integrity, and I truly appreciated that. His ability to resolve my case in under a year was a testament to his skill and dedication.

I also want to express my heartfelt gratitude to Carol, Jonathan’s secretary. Carol was always compassionate, kind, and patient. She kept me informed and at ease throughout the process, offering both professionalism and a personal touch that made a world of difference. Her consistent support made the entire experience far less stressful.

If you're looking for a legal team that is knowledgeable, efficient, and above all, genuine, I highly recommend Jonathan Minkus and Carol. In a world that can often feel harsh, they stand out as shining examples of care, professionalism, and integrity. I will forever be grateful for the hope they restored in me and the future they helped me secure.

I never felt like just another client. Mr. Minkus treated my case, and my story, with genuine care. The outcome he achieved was extraordinary, but equally meaningful was the sense of dignity he helped restore along the way.

Carol, his assistant, was equally remarkable. Her organizational skills and attention to detail ensured that all documentation and deadlines were handled flawlessly. She was always approachable, promptly addressing my questions with kindness and clarity, which made a stressful process feel manageable.

Together, they form a powerhouse team that genuinely cares about their clients’ outcomes. Their tireless efforts and personalized approach gave me hope and ultimately led to a successful result. If you’re seeking a pardon attorney who combines expertise with heartfelt advocacy, Mr. Minkus and Carol are the absolute best choice. Thank you both for changing my life!

On April 15, 2021 I have engaged this law firm to help me to get one theft felony vacated in Dupage County. Out of three criminal attorneys I contacted, this law office was the only one who told me that would be able to get the charge vacated, the other two attorneys told me that it is impossible and they have never seen it done, the prosecutors simply do not agree to vacating old guilty felony judgments, nor would they ever agree and set a precedent. Nevertheless, this law office had implicitly told me that they will get it done. I have retained his services and paid in full. I was fully aware that no lawyer can guarantee an outcome, however, being lied to and being misled is another thing. Ultimately, the charge did not get vacated, as the prosecutor did not agree to it, just like the other two attorneys have told me. I would not recommend or refer anyone to him.
reviews.ownerResponse: Post-conviction relief is not like other forms of criminal defense. An attorney must have knowledge of these intricate and specialized sets of laws. It is not surprising that other attorneys stated it couldn't be done. Mr. Minkus has over 30 years of experience and was successful at getting the criminal conviction removed for one of the family members Mr. Gorka hired him to represent. I appreciate all of your kind words and appreciation that you expressed when I was successful for your other family member and I regret that I was not successful in the other case.

reviews.text: My wife and I have tried 3 times for her green card and was rejected three times because of some trouble she was accused of before she spoke English.  I asked another Lawyer is there anything we can do and she referred us to Jonathan Minkus.  We meet with Jonathan and said he might be able to help us but it would be difficult.  Jonathan sure did what he said he could do.  He had to fly to another state and negotiate our post conviction with prosecutors and judges he never meet before and 10 years after the false conviction that she was charged with.  He worked a COMPLETE MIRICAL for my wife.  With the change in Judgement that other Lawyers we hired over the years, who were never able to do anything but charge us money and she is now eligible for her Green Card.  Thanks to Jonathon we can have a normal life without fears of deportation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Nevers, thank you for leaving a review. We understand how stressful these types of cases can be. Our immigration post-conviction lawyer works hard to give his clients the results they deserve.

reviews.text: Stay away from this ,,law office :))”this is joke not law firm.First John said he needs 7500$ for my case,as soon as i surrounded by his advice he asked another 7500$ for representing me in court,first 7500$ were money just for using his name in court he said,joke right? :)).He did nothing but shaking in court room like he was getting the time :)) anyway he just took the deal he was offered without negotiation and fu...my case really bad.After all he did not even know that i got probation time,he told me i was good to go until i got a call from probation office.So if you want your life screwed and left on the side of the rd with empty pockets this is the right place to go.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry to hear you were displeased with our service. Mr. Minkus works diligently to ensure our clients receive the best outcome possible. If there is anything further you would like to discuss, please give us a call.
